L1.area: External resource fetching, caching, and local file storage infrastructure

L2.description: A remote code injection vulnerability exists where a URL specified in a CSS stylesheet declaration controls the file extension of a locally cached resource. An attacker can cause a file with an executable extension to be written to the server's cache directory, potentially enabling arbitrary code execution on the server.

L3.area: Font metrics computation and remote font registration subsystem
L3.description: A remote code injection vulnerability exists where a URL specified in a CSS @font-face declaration controls the file extension of a locally cached font file. An attacker can cause a file with an executable extension (such as .php) to be written to the server's font cache directory by providing a specially crafted font URL, potentially enabling arbitrary remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The registerFont function derives the local cache file extension directly from the attacker-controlled remote font URL, allowing an attacker to save files with executable extensions (e.g., .php) via @font-face CSS declarations, enabling remote code execution.
